decision_log.title: Budget Estimate
decision_log.decision: Estimated ~$12,000-15,000/month across 3 environments based on 15 RFQ services and volumetrics
decision_log.rationale: RFQ does not specify an explicit budget. Estimate derived from Azure retail pricing for swedencentral region. Redis 128 GB is the dominant cost driver at 30-40% of production costs.

decision_log.title: Redis 128 GB Tier
decision_log.decision: Redis Enterprise E50 selected over Premium P4
decision_log.rationale: 34% cheaper than Premium P4 ($3,800/mo for 53 GB). E50 provides 128 GB capacity, zone redundancy, Redis modules (RedisJSON, RediSearch, RedisTimeSeries), and active geo-replication capability.

decision_log.title: Container Platform
decision_log.decision: AKS Standard selected over Azure Container Apps
decision_log.rationale: RFQ Section 4.1 #10 mandates Managed Kubernetes. 15-service microservices architecture benefits from Kubernetes-native tooling (Helm, operators, service mesh). AKS provides full VNet control and zone-redundant node pools.

decision_log.title: Edge/CDN/WAF Platform
decision_log.decision: Front Door Premium selected with EU Data Boundary risk accepted
decision_log.rationale: Integrated WAF+CDN, global performance, lower cost than App Gateway + CDN. EU Data Boundary exclusion documented as risk OQ-04; requires legal validation.

decision_log.title: Storage Redundancy
decision_log.decision: ZRS selected over GRS for GDPR compliance
decision_log.rationale: GRS replicates to paired region outside EU control — incompatible with GDPR single-region data residency constraint. ZRS provides zone-level redundancy within swedencentral.

decision_log.title: Container Platform ADR
decision_log.decision: Recorded AKS Standard as the primary container platform
decision_log.rationale: RFQ Section 4.1 #10 mandates managed Kubernetes and the Step 2 assessment selected AKS for the microservices platform baseline.

decision_log.title: Caching Tier ADR
decision_log.decision: Recorded Redis Enterprise E50 as the primary caching tier
decision_log.rationale: E50 provides native 128 GB capacity and lower cost than the Premium clustered alternative documented in the Step 2 assessment.

decision_log.title: EU Data Boundary ADR
decision_log.decision: Established Application Gateway as the compliant baseline and restricted Entra MFA to EU-sovereign methods
decision_log.choice: Application Gateway baseline, FIDO2/passkey/TOTP MFA, tenant EU Data Boundary prerequisite
decision_log.rationale: Azure Front Door is excluded from the EU Data Boundary, Entra MFA sovereignty has method-specific restrictions, and tenant-level EU Data Boundary configuration must be in place before subscription creation.

decision_log.title: Template Governance Baseline
decision_log.decision: Generate an unverified governance template baseline for the automated E2E run.
decision_log.choice: Template-based governance artifacts with no Azure credential dependency
decision_log.rationale: The E2E workflow requires Step 3.5 artifacts even when live Azure Policy discovery cannot run. The output is explicitly marked unverified and must be replaced with live policy data before deployment.

decision_log.title: Deployment Strategy
decision_log.decision: Phased deployment with 4 stages: Foundation → Data → Edge → Platform
decision_log.rationale: Complex architecture with 17 resource types, >5 resources, production/compliance workload. Phased deployment ensures each layer is validated before dependent resources are deployed. Approval gates between phases for governance compliance.

decision_log.title: Application Gateway over Front Door
decision_log.decision: Application Gateway WAF v2 selected as ingress baseline instead of Front Door Premium
decision_log.choice: Application Gateway WAF v2 (Prevention mode, OWASP 3.2)
decision_log.rationale: ADR-003 established Application Gateway as the EU Data Boundary compliant baseline. Front Door is excluded from EU Data Boundary. POL-015 requires regional WAF ingress with Application Gateway WAF v2 in Prevention mode. App Gateway is a regional service keeping all data processing within swedencentral.

decision_log.title: AVM Module Coverage
decision_log.decision: 16 of 17 resource categories use Azure Verified Modules after refreshing Step 4 against the live AVM catalog.
decision_log.choice: AVM-first with raw Bicep only for Budget; diagnostic settings now use the AVM helper module
decision_log.rationale: A refreshed AVM catalog lookup confirmed newer versions for all planned resource modules and showed that `avm/res/insights/diagnostic-setting` is now available. The corrected Step 4 plan therefore keeps only `Microsoft.Consumption/budgets` as a non-AVM resource.

decision_log.title: Live Azure Policy Discovery
decision_log.decision: Replaced the template governance baseline with live ARM REST discovery results for the sandbox subscription.
decision_log.rationale: The user requested read-only live governance discovery. ARM REST queries succeeded and showed that the real blockers are narrower than the placeholder baseline, with tag compliance becoming the primary immediate blocker and a live tag-key drift between resource-group deny and resource modify policies.

decision_log.title: Step 4 Self-Correction Against Live Governance
decision_log.decision: Refreshed the implementation plan and AVM matrix to use the live 9-tag governance baseline, cumulative stage semantics, updated AVM versions, and forecast-only budget notifications with explicit recipients.
decision_log.choice: Portable plan contract with live governance authority and 16/17 AVM coverage
decision_log.rationale: The original Step 4 artifact retained stale placeholder assumptions from before live policy discovery. The corrected plan removes the 4-tag contract, preempts storage modify-policy drift, fixes phase determinism, aligns environment statements to the approved architecture, and reflects current AVM availability including diagnostic settings.

open_findings.description: RFQ does not specify explicit budget — estimate ~$12K-15K/month requires validation
open_findings.resolution: Validate with Azure Pricing MCP in Step 2

open_findings.description: The 128 GB cache baseline is now fixed to Azure Managed Redis Enterprise E50 unless later commercial review explicitly changes it.
open_findings.resolution: Resolved in Step 2 and propagated through Steps 1, 4, 5, and 6.

open_findings.description: Regional ingress requires Azure DNS ownership and certificate sourcing. Global CDN or Front Door remains exception-only.
open_findings.resolution: Resolved in the corrected baseline: Application Gateway WAF v2 plus Azure DNS is the default; any global edge service requires written approval.

open_findings.description: Azure Front Door is a global service excluded from EU Data Boundary
open_findings.resolution: Resolved in Step 3 baseline: Application Gateway is the compliant default; Front Door requires written exception

open_findings.description: Multi-region DR excluded from RFQ scope — reassess for Release 2.0
GitHub vs Azure DevOps — RFQ does not specify preference
Step 3.5 governance artifacts are template-based and unverified because Azure credentials were unavailable during the automated E2E run.
open_findings.resolution: Resolved on 2026-04-02 by replacing the governance artifacts with live Azure Policy discovery results and aligning the downstream Step 4, Step 5, and Step 6 artifacts to the live 9-tag baseline.
